title: SGF Receipts Estimates for FY 2019 through FY 2021

key_numbers.label: Growth Rate of Individual Income Tax
Growth Rate of Corporation Income Tax
Growth Rate of Retail Sales Tax
Growth Rate of Compensating Use Tax

evidence.quote: The growth rates of the four highest generating revenue sources reflect the latest assumptions about the impact of recent changes in state and federal law:
The estimate for total taxes was increased by $291.2 million, while the estimate for non-tax sources was increased $15.1 million.
Receipts from this source exceeded the final FY 2018 estimate by $62.4 million; and had been running $36.2 million above the prior FY 2019 estimate through October.

About $25.6 million of state retail sales and compensating use taxes ($21.5 million from the State General Fund and $4.1 million from the State Highway Fund) was returned to pay the STAR bonds from ten separate districts.
